name: azure-resource-manager-durabletask-dotnet
description: Azure Resource Manager SDK for Durable Task Scheduler management in .NET
capabilities: Create and manage Durable Task Scheduler resources in Azure
Configure task hubs under schedulers for orchestration workflows
Set up retention policies for orchestration history
Manage SKU selection between Dedicated and Consumption options
Handle IP allowlists for network security configuration
Perform CRUD operations on Azure Resource Manager Durable Task resources
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected 0 security issues. The skill provides reference documentation for Azure Resource Manager SDK usage for Durable Task Scheduler management. No prompt injection attempts or malicious patterns detected.
valueStatement: Manage Azure Durable Task Scheduler resources efficiently using the Azure Resource Manager SDK. This skill guides you through creating schedulers, task hubs, and retention policies with proper authentication and best practices.
userTitle: Provision Azure Durable Task Schedulers with .NET
limitations: Management plane only - does not start orchestrations or query instances
Requires Azure subscription and appropriate RBAC permissions
Data plane operations require separate Microsoft.DurableTask.Client.AzureManaged package
Long-running operations require proper WaitUntil handling

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Provisioning Infrastructure
useCases.description: Automate the creation of Durable Task Scheduler resources as part of infrastructure deployment pipelines

useCases.title: Application Developer Setting Up Orchestrations
useCases.description: Provision the required Azure resources before deploying orchestration code to production

useCases.title: Cloud Architect Designing Solutions
useCases.description: Evaluate and configure Durable Task Scheduler options for enterprise workflow solutions

promptTemplates.title: Beginner: Create a basic scheduler
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a Durable Task Scheduler in my Azure subscription using the Azure.ResourceManager.DurableTask SDK. I have my subscription ID and resource group ready.
promptTemplates.scenario: First-time setup of Durable Task infrastructure

promptTemplates.title: Beginner: List existing schedulers
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to list all Durable Task Schedulers in my Azure subscription and display their properties.
promptTemplates.scenario: Inventory and discovery of existing resources

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate: Configure scheduler with IP restrictions
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to create a Durable Task Scheduler with IP allowlist restrictions for production security. Show me the complete code with proper error handling.
promptTemplates.scenario: Production deployment with network security requirements

promptTemplates.title: Advanced: Full lifecycle management
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete example that provisions a Durable Task Scheduler with Dedicated SKU, creates a task hub, configures retention policy, and includes cleanup code for resource deletion.

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for authentication to support multiple credential types including managed identity
Always use WaitUntil.Completed for operations that must finish before dependent actions proceed
Delete task hubs before deleting their parent schedulers to avoid resource conflicts
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode credentials or connection strings in source code
Avoid using WaitUntil.Started without implementing proper polling logic
Do not skip error handling for RequestFailedException when calling ARM APIs

faq.question: What is the difference between management plane and data plane for Durable Task?
faq.answer: Management plane (this SDK) handles resource provisioning like creating schedulers and task hubs. Data plane handles runtime operations like starting orchestrations and querying instances.

faq.question: Which SKU should I choose for my Durable Task Scheduler?
faq.answer: Use Dedicated SKU for production workloads requiring predictable performance. Use Consumption SKU for development or variable workloads that benefit from serverless auto-scaling.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure Resource Manager?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which automatically tries multiple authentication methods including managed identity, Azure CLI credentials, and environment variables.

faq.question: Can I update a scheduler after creation?
faq.answer: Yes, use CreateOrUpdateAsync to modify scheduler properties like SKU capacity or IP allowlist. The operation is idempotent and safe to call multiple times.

faq.question: What happens if I delete a scheduler that has task hubs?
faq.answer: You must delete all task hubs under a scheduler before deleting the scheduler itself. Attempting to delete a scheduler with existing task hubs will fail.

faq.question: How do I handle long-running operations in the SDK?
faq.answer: Use WaitUntil.Completed to block until the operation finishes, or WaitUntil.Started to begin the operation and poll for completion manually using the returned operation object.
